primerTitle: Risk glossary — how to use it
primer:
## Risk glossary
The canonical vocabulary of the Banking Risk curriculum — the terms every later deck assumes. Grouped by domain:
- **Foundations & measures** — risk types, appetite, capital, VaR/ES, backtesting.
- **Market risk** — the Greeks, DV01, FRTB, VaR methods.
- **Credit risk** — PD/LGD/EAD, RWA, IRB, IFRS 9.
- **Counterparty & XVA** — exposure measures, the XVA stack, margin, CCPs.
- **Liquidity & ALM** — HQLA, LCR/NSFR, IRRBB, EVE/NII.
- **Capital & regulation** — CET1/AT1/T2, buffers, SREP, Basel, the authorities.
- **Operational, model & stress** — event types, SMA, model risk, stress testing.

Clear this first — the rest of the course builds on these definitions. Each card defines one term; use the category chips to focus a domain.
